sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The ssml_mc20 class is a specialization of the event_template and is designed to handle events from the Same-Sign-Muon-Lepton analysis for Run 3 (MC20). It defines the structure of the event, including the particles and their relationships, and provides methods for building and compiling the event from the input data.

sect1.title: The ssml_mc20 Class
sect1.para: The ssml_mc20 class inherits from event_template and implements its virtual methods to provide event-specific functionality.

listitem.para: ssml_mc20(): The constructor initializes the event by setting its name, defining the data leaves to be read from the input tree, and registering the particle types that are expected in the event.
~ssml_mc20(): The destructor cleans up any resources allocated by the event.
event_template* clone(): Creates a new instance of the ssml_mc20 event.
void build(element_t* el): Populates the event's data members (event number, weights, MET, etc.) from the input data element.

void CompileEvent(): This is the core method of the class. It reconstructs the event by linking the various particles together. This includes:
Associating tops with their parent Z' boson.
Linking partons to their corresponding jets, truth jets, muons, and electrons.
Matching various particle collections (partons, electrons, muons, jets, truth jets) to their parent tops.
Vectorizing the particle collections for easier access.

sect2.title: Event Attributes
sect2.para:
The ssml_mc20 event contains a wide range of attributes, including:
Event Information: eventNumber, event_category.
Weights: weight_mc, weight_pileup, weight_jvt_effSF, weight_lep_tightSF, weight_ftag_effSF, weight_beamspot.
Kinematic Variables: HT_all, met, phi, met_sum.
Selection Flags: pass_ssee, pass_ssem, pass_ssmm, pass_eem_zveto, etc.
Particle Counts: n_electrons, n_fjets, n_jets, n_leptons, n_muons.

sect2.title: Particle Collections
sect2.para:
The ssml_mc20 event contains the following particle collections:
m_zprime: A map of Z' bosons.
m_tops: A map of top quarks.
m_truthjets: A map of truth jets.
m_partons: A map of partons.
m_leptons: A map of leptons.
m_electrons: A map of electrons.
m_muons: A map of muons.
m_jets: A map of jets.

After CompileEvent() is called, these maps are used to populate the following vector collections for easier access:
Tops
Zprime
Detector (a combined collection of jets, muons, and electrons)
Leptons (a combined collection of muons and electrons)
Leptsn (a collection of lepton objects)
Electrons
